title: Prompt Injection and Task Hijacking
summary: Malicious instructions embedded in web pages, tool outputs, emails, URLs, or third-party content redirect the agent away from the user objective.

title: Tool Metadata and MCP Poisoning
summary: Attacks that poison tool descriptions, MCP metadata, manifests, names, or response channels before or during tool selection and invocation.

title: Dependency and Supply Chain Poisoning
summary: Malicious packages, dependency confusion, poisoned updates, build-time compromise, or install-time payloads affecting skills, plugins, or agent runtimes.

title: Config and Parameter Poisoning
summary: Poisoning system prompts, tool schemas, argument defaults, preference-learning data, PEFT adapters, or hidden backdoor triggers that alter downstream behavior.

title: Memory and Retrieval Poisoning
summary: Poisoned memories, RAG stores, prior plans, or retrieved artifacts persist across turns and influence future tool use.

title: High-Privilege Tool Misuse
summary: Unauthorized or unsafe actions via tools with real-world side effects, especially in OS, browser, finance, healthcare, legal, or infra contexts.

title: Harmful Task Misuse and Jailbreaks
summary: Benchmarks where the user or environment attempts to coerce the agent into harmful multi-step behavior while preserving capability.

title: Multi-Agent Adversarial Manipulation
summary: One compromised agent or message channel manipulates peer agents to perform unsafe actions.

title: Benchmark Coverage and Meta-Audit
summary: Benchmarks or audit frameworks that test the completeness and blind spots of existing agent-safety suites.
